steps.title: Identify the list of examples
steps.content: The sentence gives examples of places where mosaics are found: “places such as ______ stations, and public squares.” The blank needs to complete a list of place examples.

steps.title: Apply the rule for listing items
steps.content:
When you list multiple items (A, B, and C), separate them with commas.

Choice D creates “libraries, train stations, and public squares”—three properly separated places.

Choice A uses a colon instead of a comma (wrong for lists), choice C uses a dash (not standard), and choice B has no punctuation between “libraries” and “train,” making it read as one awkward unit.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
